name: Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón
region_intro: Mountain biking around Santa Eulalia de Oscos offers diverse terrain within the Oscos-Eo region of western Asturias. The area features a captivating mountainous landscape, abundant with dense forests, clear rivers, and picturesque waterfalls. As part of a World Biosphere Reserve, the region provides a network of trails traversing verdant valleys, rolling mountains, and traditional villages. The mountainous orography is ideal for mountain biking, with routes often following forest tracks and offering views of the valleys and rivers. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Eulalia De Oscos The most popular mountain bike trail is Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete, offering challenging climbs through forested areas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón, a moderate 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path that winds through the Biosphere Reserve, featuring river sections and a notable waterfall. Local mountain bikers also love the Acebo Viewpoint – A Fonsagrada loop from Barbeitos, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Eulalia de Oscos is defined by dense forests, river valleys, and rolling mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult, demanding routes. The routes in Santa Eulalia de Oscos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 28 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Eulalia de Oscos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Santa Eulalia de Oscos?
A: There are 7 mountain bike trails available around Santa Eulalia de Oscos, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Santa Eulalia de Oscos?
A: Santa Eulalia de Oscos is part of a World Biosphere Reserve, characterized by dense forests and rivers. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, the region's natural beauty suggests spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder, potentially wetter conditions, especially in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: The available routes around Santa Eulalia de Oscos are primarily classified as moderate or difficult. For a moderate option that might be suitable for more experienced families, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211504216/cascada-de-seimeira-de-murias-desde-pumares-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.0 km long and winds through the Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Santa Eulalia de Oscos?
A: The region features a captivating mountainous landscape with diverse terrain. You can expect trails to traverse dense forests, follow clear rivers, and wind through verdant valleys and rolling mountains. Many routes utilize forest tracks, offering spectacular views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482692/grandas-de-salime-pasando-por-el-rio-vavia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 37.3 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211504216/cascada-de-seimeira-de-murias-desde-pumares-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.0 km circular path.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, including dense forests, crystal-clear rivers, and picturesque waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211504216/cascada-de-seimeira-de-murias-desde-pumares-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, features a notable waterfall. You can also find routes that offer spectacular views of valleys and rivers, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebo Viewpoint – A Fonsagrada loop from Barbeitos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Eulalia de Oscos?
A: The trails in Santa Eulalia de Oscos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs through forested areas, and the scenic routes that wind through the Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region offers cultural and historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazonovo Mill Museum&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century hydraulic mallet, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4524552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Bárbara de Silvela&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features traditional villages with distinctive stone and black slate architecture.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211504216/cascada-de-seimeira-de-murias-desde-pumares-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that winds through the Biosphere Reserve and features a notable waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a nearby highlight.

Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain biking in Santa Eulalia de Oscos?
A: Mountain biking in Santa Eulalia de Oscos offers a range of difficulties. Out of the 7 available routes, 2 are classified as moderate, and 5 are considered difficult. This means you can find options for both intermediate and advanced riders looking for a challenge.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: As part of a World Biosphere Reserve, the dense forests of the Oscos-Eo region are home to a variety of wildlife. You might encounter roe deer, wild boar, and foxes while exploring the trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, there are several challenging long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pumares Village – Santa Eulalia de Oscos loop from Santa Eulalia de Oscos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.0 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Oscos Mill – Santa Eulalia de Oscos loop from Venta Nova&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option spanning 45.2 km.
tips: Santa Eulalia de Oscos (Santalla d'Ozcos in Asturian) is a town located on the banks of the Agüeira River. It has interesting ethnographic museums, such as the Museo Casa Natal del Marqués de Sargadelos, the promoter of ceramic production. You can also visit the Irene Villar artisan loom, and, a few kilometers from the city center, the old Mazonovo blacksmith shop.
The village of Pumares, in Santa Eulalia de Oscos, is a small village of stone, black slate and water.
In the Carballido Natural Area is this magnificent waterfall of the Augüeira River with approximately 30 meters of fall where the water rushes between trees until it reaches the pool.
